pages.full_text:
[Printed Eden Project leaflet, no handwritten text. Aerial photograph at top showing the Eden Project site with printed labels: "Rainforest Biome", "Covered Biome Entr[ance]", "Mediterranean Bio[me]", "Stage", "Link", "Land train", "Outdoor Biome". Below, printed sections:

__Stage__
The home of our seasonal events programme:
- Den building
- Ice skating
- Concerts (Eden Sessions).

__Outdoor Biome__
Ten years ago this Biome (with the sky for a roof) was a barren landscape, with no soil and no plants. Today find crops that provide your foods, fuels, medicines and materials. Explore our wild places too and consider why we need to conserve them.

__Rainforest Biome__
The largest rainforest in captivity. Climb to the top on the Rainforest Lookout, find out how rainforests keep us alive and how to help conserve them. This Biome is also about trade and connections: find a smallholding which grows cocoa for chocolate, compare the Malaysian back garden to your own. These places are far away but we're connected.

__Link__
(covered Biome [entrance])
This grass-roofed [building] is the entrance [to both] covered Biomes [—] it helps keep in [heat]. You'll find loos, [a] membership de[sk], delicious food [at] Eden Bakery.]
pages.notes: This page is a printed Eden Project visitor leaflet glued into the diary — no handwritten content. The aerial photo at the top has printed labels. The text is entirely printed, not handwritten. No handwritten page number visible. The leaflet references 'Ten years ago' for the Outdoor Biome but no absolute year is given on this page. Eden Project opened in 2001, so 'ten years ago' would suggest c.2011, consistent with volume B covering 2003-2019.

pages.full_text:
[Printed Eden Project leaflet continued, no handwritten text. Top: aerial photograph continuation with printed labels: "Lift and Bridge", "Core", "Visitor Centre", "[Ra]inforest Biome Entrance", "[Med]iterranean Biome". Left column contains partial text from the cut-off Link and Visitor Centre sections (continuation from L page). Centre column:

__Mediterranean Biome__
Sights, scents and stories from the Mediterranean, South Africa and California. Discover [text continues beyond cut]

[Printed numbered location list in a brown/tan box:]

__Rainforest Biome__
1 Tropical Islands
2 West Africa
3 Tropical South America
4 Rainforest Lookout

__Mediterranean Biome__

28 Metals, minerals and mine[s]

__The Core__

__The Visitor Centre__
34 Shop

36 Apple Cafe
37 Driftwood horse

__The Link__
38 The Eden Bakery

Right column:
__Core__
Our education, arts and events hub. Home to 'the Seed' our massive [sculp]ture, our schools [prog]rammes and a range [of exh]ibitions. Try out th[e] [world]'s largest nutcracke[r] [and] 'services' that [we] provide (includ[ing] [climate] control) and [disco]ver some of [the] [many] ways in which [com]munities can adapt [to] the needs of the 21st century. Delicious food in Jo's Cafe too.]
pages.notes: Continuation of the same printed Eden Project visitor leaflet. No handwritten content. No handwritten page number. The printed numbered list is a directory of all Eden Project zones/exhibits. Some text on right and left edges is cropped/cut off. There appear to be two items numbered 31 (WEEE man and Jo's Cafe) — this matches the printed original.
